attacking_style: Opportunistic counter-attacker who capitalizes on GC battles and breakaway confusion. Strikes in final climbs when favorites are distracted or fixated on each other, not from long range.
team_tactics: Benefits from family/team sacrifices in breakaways (brother Dylan's 10km+ pull). Operates independently once positioned, minimal visible lead-out but relies on smart pre-positioning.
terrain_preference: Final climbs of hilly/medium mountain stages where GC riders neutralize each other. Excels when race dynamics create tactical confusion rather than pure climbing tests.
vulnerability: Pure climbing power against top GC riders (lost 12 seconds to Yates over 4km). Not a dominant force; requires tactical circumstances to align rather than imposing his own will.
key_patterns: Wins from breakaways when peloton focused on GC dynamics, not chasing escapees (2 instances)
Counter-attacks at optimal moments rather than initiating long-range moves (3 instances)
Leverages familial/team support for crucial positioning before final attacks (2 documented)
Targets stages where GC favorites neutralize each other, creating stage hunter opportunities (2 instances)
form_trajectory: positive: 2020 Vuelta win shows continued ability to read races and execute; consistent tactical intelligence across all observations
